title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Findings** - **High**: `Codex -> Kernel` adapter is missing because [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md) does not exist. Goal-state symmetry is broken. - **High**: [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md#L40) is not a thin router yet; it still carries heavy operational/detail sections (`Kernel Orchestration`, fallback quorum details, publication/GHA/channel/legal blocks through [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md#L107)). This defeats minimal always-load. - **High**: [CLAUDE.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CLAUDE.md#L13) still falls back to heavy [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/AGENTS.md), making `~/.claude/AGENTS.md` an active runtime dependency contrary to the target. - **Medium**: Provider mapping should be launch-source explicit for peer-orchestrator clarity. Current wording in [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md#L32) is session-based; recommend explicit `launched from Claude Code -> FUGUE` / `launched from Codex -> Kernel`. - **Medium**: If FUGUE/KERNEL contracts are absorbed into adapters, invariants must be moved with them; otherwise domain gates and no-cross-substitution rules can regress. Current gates are split across [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md#L35), [CLAUDE.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CLAUDE.md#L26), and [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/AGENTS.md#L61). - **Medium**: [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/AGENTS.md#L38) uses `Main Engine/Sub Engine` labels under FUGUE, which risks hierarchy misunderstanding against the peer model. - **Low**: No guardrail test is visible for this architecture (e.g., required adapter files, forbidden heavy sections in always-load files, launch-source mapping), so drift risk is high.
